title: matelea violacea
intro: Matelea violacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbanes or olives, although it is not closely related to either. It is a woody vine native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is valued for its medicinal properties. The plant contains various alkaloids, including violaceine, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation. Matelea violacea is used in herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments, including fever, wounds, and respiratory issues.
